data.body: DONE (config-loop, commit 6794eeb1). Wired the documented CLI precedence layer via a git -c style global override: rebar -c SECTION.KEY=VALUE <subcommand> (repeatable, before the subcommand) installs the highest-precedence cli layer for EVERY config consumer this invocation (verify gate, push/pull, display mode, compact, mcp). Implementation: config._CLI_OVERRIDES process-global + set_cli_overrides (clears result cache) + parse_cli_overrides (SECTION.KEY=VALUE -> nested sparse dict, ConfigError on missing = / non-dotted key); load_config + resolve_with_sources use effective_cli = explicit-arg-or-global for BOTH the cache key and resolution (no stale-cache gap; explicit {} opts out of the global); reset_config_cache nulls the global (test isolation; library/MCP never set it). main() parses leading -c/--config/--config= and fails cleanly (exit 1) on malformed before any command runs; only leading tokens consumed (a subcommand’s own -c untouched). AC1 (a real CLI flag routes through cli_overrides + beats env+file): rebar -c sync.push=off beats REBAR_SYNC_PUSH env AND a [sync] config file — tested end-to-end + unit. AC2 (rebar config shows cli provenance): rebar config -c k=v tags the value [cli] — tested. AC3 (full suite green): 1985 passed; ruff check+format clean. Subagent review: ship-ready, no critical/major; precedence/cache correct, verify gate fail-closed on a malformed/bad-value -c, library/MCP isolation confirmed. docs/config.md updated.
